seoTitle: How to Log In to Microsoft Excel Across Devices
metaDescription: Sign into Microsoft Excel on desktop, mobile, or the web by entering your account credentials and clearing any required multi-factor verification.
intro: Getting stuck on licensing errors or being blocked from editing cloud files usually comes down to not being properly signed in. This guide covers signing into Excel from the desktop app, the mobile app, and the web portal, plus handling multi-factor verification along the way.

steps.heading: Open Account settings on desktop
steps.body: Launch Excel, click the File tab, and select Account in the sidebar.

steps.heading: Click Sign In
steps.body: Click the Sign In button under your product information.

steps.heading: Enter your credentials
steps.body: Type your personal or corporate Microsoft account email, then enter your password and complete any multi-factor code.

steps.heading: Sign in on mobile
steps.body: Open the Excel app on your phone, tap your profile icon, and select Sign In to enter your credentials.

steps.heading: Use the web portal as an alternative
steps.body: Go to the official Microsoft 365 web portal and sign in to access cloud sheets directly in your browser.

steps.heading: Confirm the sync
steps.body: Check that your avatar photo displays in the application ribbon to confirm you're fully signed in across devices.

faq.question: Can I use the same account across desktop, mobile, and web?
faq.answer: Yes, Microsoft's unified identity system is designed to let you use the same credentials across all three environments.

faq.question: What if I have both a personal and work account?
faq.answer: You can switch between personal and enterprise profiles, though you'll need to manage which one is active to avoid losing your layouts.

faq.question: Do I need multi-factor authentication every time I sign in?
faq.answer: This depends on your account and organization's security policies, since some setups require it more frequently than others.

faq.question: Is the web version free to sign into without a paid license?
faq.answer: Yes, the Microsoft 365 web portal offers free access to Excel for the web with just a basic Microsoft account.
